claims.claim_text: The CKA is a hands-on, performance-based command-line exam, and Storage is a named exam domain worth 10%.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 3
claims.rationale: The CNCF CKA page explicitly describes the exam as performance-based, command-line, and lists Storage at 10%.

claims.claim_text: `emptyDir` is created when a Pod is assigned to a node, survives container crashes within that Pod, and is deleted when the Pod is removed from the node.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. Build the Storage Decision Model Before Touching YAML, paragraphs 2-3
claims.rationale: The Volumes doc directly states the `emptyDir` lifecycle and its behavior across container crashes.

claims.claim_text: A memory-backed `emptyDir` uses tmpfs, and bytes written there count against the memory limit of the writing container.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. Build the Storage Decision Model Before Touching YAML, paragraph 4
claims.rationale: The Volumes doc explicitly describes `emptyDir.medium: Memory` as tmpfs and notes that writes count against container memory.

claims.claim_text: ConfigMap or Secret volumes can update from source object changes, but a `subPath` mount does not receive those live updates.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. Build the Storage Decision Model Before Touching YAML, projected volumes paragraph
claims.rationale: The Volumes doc explicitly notes that ConfigMap and Secret `subPath` mounts do not receive updates.

claims.claim_text: `hostPath` mounts part of the node filesystem into a Pod and presents significant security risks.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. Build the Storage Decision Model Before Touching YAML, hostPath paragraph
claims.rationale: The Volumes doc warns that `hostPath` exposes the host filesystem and carries many security risks.

claims.claim_text: Under the Pod Security Standards Baseline policy, `hostPath` volumes must be forbidden.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. Build the Storage Decision Model Before Touching YAML, hostPath paragraph tail
claims.rationale: The Pod Security Standards page explicitly lists `hostPath` volumes as forbidden in the Baseline policy.

claims.claim_text: PVs are cluster resources, PVCs are namespaced claims, and a Pod can only use a claim that exists in the same namespace as the Pod.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. Build the Storage Decision Model Before Touching YAML, persistent storage paragraph and diagram explanation
claims.rationale: The Persistent Volumes doc states that PVs are cluster resources and claims must exist in the same namespace as the Pod using them.

claims.claim_text: Static PV binding usually selects the smallest matching volume that satisfies the request.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Understand Binding as a Contract, Not a Coincidence, capacity binding paragraph
claims.anchor_text: Static PV binding usually selects the smallest matching volume that satisfies the request, because using a much larger volume wastes capacity.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Static PV binding requires a matching volume that satisfies the request, and binding a much larger volume can waste capacity.
claims.rationale: Kubernetes docs say a matching PV must satisfy the claim and may exceed the requested size, but they do not promise a smallest-match selection rule.

claims.claim_text: `WaitForFirstConsumer` delays PVC binding or provisioning until a Pod uses the claim, so Kubernetes can take the Pod's scheduling constraints into account.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Understand Binding as a Contract, Not a Coincidence, volumeBindingMode paragraph
claims.rationale: The StorageClass doc directly explains `WaitForFirstConsumer` and its topology-aware scheduling behavior.

claims.claim_text: With `Retain`, deleting the PVC leaves the PV in a released state, not available for another claim, because the previous claimant's data remains until an administrator reclaims it.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Understand Binding as a Contract, Not a Coincidence, reclaimPolicy paragraphs
claims.rationale: The Persistent Volumes doc explicitly describes `Retain`, `Released`, and manual reclamation.

claims.claim_text: For supported plugins, `Delete` removes both the PV object and the backing storage asset, and dynamically provisioned volumes inherit the StorageClass reclaim policy, which defaults to `Delete`.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Understand Binding as a Contract, Not a Coincidence, reclaimPolicy paragraphs; section: Hands-On Exercise Step 5
claims.rationale: The Persistent Volumes doc directly states the `Delete` reclaim behavior and the default inheritance from StorageClass.

claims.claim_text: A PVC with no `storageClassName` may use the cluster default StorageClass, while `storageClassName: ""` explicitly disables dynamic provisioning and only matches PVs with no class.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Understand Binding as a Contract, Not a Coincidence, storageClassName paragraph; section: Did You Know item 1
claims.rationale: The Persistent Volumes doc explicitly distinguishes omitted `storageClassName` from the empty-string form.

claims.claim_text: `ReadWriteOnce` means read-write mount from a single node, and it can still allow multiple Pods on that same node; multi-node access depends on access modes and storage backend support.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. Understand Binding as a Contract, Not a Coincidence, access mode paragraph
claims.rationale: The Persistent Volumes doc explicitly defines `ReadWriteOnce` as single-node and notes that multiple Pods on the same node may still access it.

claims.claim_text: PVC cloning uses another PVC as the `dataSource`, is only available for CSI dynamic provisioners, and requires source and destination PVCs to be in the same namespace.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Use Snapshots and Clones as Recovery Tools, opening paragraphs
claims.rationale: The CSI Volume Cloning doc directly states the `dataSource` model, CSI dependency, and same-namespace requirement.

claims.claim_text: VolumeSnapshot support is implemented with CRDs, is only available for CSI drivers, and depends on the snapshot controller and related components being installed.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Use Snapshots and Clones as Recovery Tools, opening and troubleshooting paragraphs
claims.rationale: The Volume Snapshots doc explicitly says the API objects are CRDs, snapshot support is CSI-only, and controller-side components are required.

claims.claim_text: Restoring from a snapshot creates a new PVC from a `VolumeSnapshot`; Kubernetes does not restore an existing PVC in place.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Use Snapshots and Clones as Recovery Tools, restore paragraph
claims.rationale: The official Kubernetes snapshot GA post explicitly says snapshot restore provisions a new volume and does not support reverting an existing PVC.

claims.claim_text: The Kubernetes Snapshot API does not provide application consistency guarantees, so applications may need quiescing or flush steps before snapshotting.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Use Snapshots and Clones as Recovery Tools, consistency paragraph
claims.rationale: The official Kubernetes snapshot GA post explicitly states that the Snapshot API provides no application consistency guarantees.

claims.claim_text: `fsGroup` can make mounted files group-accessible for a non-root workload when the volume type or CSI driver supports that behavior.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. Connect Pod Symptoms to Storage Events, fsGroup paragraph; Quiz Q7 answer
claims.rationale: The Security Context task page documents `fsGroup` effects and notes CSI-driver-specific support for ownership and permission handling.

further_reading.title: Volumes | Kubernetes
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for `emptyDir`, projected volumes, `hostPath`, and general Pod volume behavior.

further_reading.title: Persistent Volumes | Kubernetes
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for PV/PVC lifecycle, access modes, reclaim policies, and `storageClassName` behavior.

further_reading.title: Storage Classes | Kubernetes
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for dynamic provisioning and `WaitForFirstConsumer` topology-aware binding.

further_reading.title: Volume Snapshots | Kubernetes
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for snapshot objects, controller requirements, and restore workflows.

notes: No shared citation pool currently exists for k8s/cka/part4-storage, so each supported claim carries its own proposed URL. Repeated factual points in Did You Know, Common Mistakes, quiz answers, and hands-on steps were deduplicated where the same citation can be attached earlier in the module body.
